message:
feat(core,vfs,fuse): plumb RequestScope through all FsOps methods for MVCC read/write scoping

Thread RequestScope through every FsOps trait method (getattr, lookup,
readdir, read, readlink, statfs, write, create, mkdir, unlink, rmdir,
rename, link, symlink, fallocate, setattr, flush, fsync, fsyncdir,
setxattr, removexattr) and all internal read helpers (read_group_desc,
read_inode, read_file_data, read_dir, lookup_name, resolve_path, etc.).

RequestScope.tx now holds an Option<ffs_mvcc::Transaction> instead of
Option<TxnId>, enabling proper MVCC lifecycle management:
- read_block_with_scope checks scope.tx staged writes, then
  scope.snapshot for MVCC-visible blocks, then raw device
- ext4_write stages block writes into scope.tx when a transaction
  is present
- begin_request_scope splits into read vs write paths
- end_request_scope handles snapshot release (reads) and transaction
  abort (uncommitted writes)
- New commit_request_scope and RequestScope::commit_if_write methods

The FUSE layer's with_request_scope closure now receives &mut
RequestScope and all mutation dispatch methods call
commit_request_scope after successful operations.

with_empty_scope convenience wrappers added on OpenFs for internal
callers that don't yet have a live scope.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
